---
title: "Phase II"
---

Phase II pages collect stable spheres, periodicity, bundles, boundary/cobordism seeds, and proof-carrying glyphs.

::: {.learning-unit}

## What To Learn Here

Phase II is the infrastructure layer. It asks how the circle-first system can support stable patterns, bundle-like data, boundary orientation, and proof-carrying glyphs without letting diagrams outrun formal status.

:::

<div class="warning-box">
Phase II pages mix proved finite seeds with planned theory-building infrastructure. The theorem cards and generated target indexes determine what is proved, planned, exploratory, or future work.
</div>

## Lesson Path

1. [Stable Spheres](stable_sphere_calculus.qmd): repeated suspension as a finite checked pattern.
2. [Bott And Clifford](bott_clifford_periodicity.qmd): period-eight clock facts as a seed, not full periodicity.
3. [Bundles](bundle_calculus.qmd): trivial-bundle projections and base-preserving transition seeds.
4. [Boundary Cobordism](boundary_cobordism.qmd): directed boundary facts.
5. [Proof-Carrying Glyphs](proof_carrying_glyphs.qmd): metadata and certificate structure for proof-linked diagrams.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why should proof-carrying glyphs point to theorem manifests instead of carrying their own unverified proof-status claims?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2S-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2BU-T0007"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="P2G-T0004"></div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
